basics.name: Glen Muthoka Mutinda
basics.headline: Final-Year BEng EEE · Embedded Systems · IC Design · Cybersecurity

summary.title: Professional Summary
summary.content: Final-year Electrical & Electronics Engineering student at the University of Southampton with specialisation in embedded systems, IC design, and cybersecurity. Maintain 69 GitHub repositories across public open source, private work, and forks. Proven track record in hardware and software development, from Ring Oscillator design for real-time clock chips at TSMC 65nm to real-time FPGA systems and full-stack applications. Experience includes space-grade software for lunar CubeSat missions and AI-driven optical authentication research. Seeking graduate opportunities in embedded systems engineering, digital design, or firmware development.

items.company: ARTEMIS Small Sat-1 Lunar CubeSat Project, University of Southampton
items.description:
• Developed and optimised flight software in C/C++ for lunar mission CubeSat, reducing system latency by 6% through algorithm optimisation and code refactoring.
  • Enhanced system reliability by 20% through implementation of comprehensive unit testing frameworks and automated debugging procedures.
  • Collaborated with multidisciplinary team of 8 engineers to deliver space-grade software meeting strict timing and safety requirements.
  • Conducted code reviews and documentation following NASA coding standards and MISRA C guidelines.
  • Implemented real-time telemetry processing system for ground station communication.

items.company: School of Electronics and Computer Science, University of Southampton
items.description:
• Represented undergraduate students on the Belonging, Inclusion, Diversity, and Equality (BIDE) Committee within ECS.
  • Gathered and communicated student perspectives on inclusivity, curriculum accessibility, and the learning environment to faculty and school leadership.
  • Collaborated with academic staff, postgraduate representatives, and the wider university EDI infrastructure to develop and review school-level initiatives.
  • Attended regular committee meetings, preparing and presenting written summaries of student feedback for formal discussion and action.

items.degree: BEng Electrical & Electronics Engineering (Expected First Class Honours)
items.description:
Key Modules: Digital Systems Design, Integrated Circuit Design, Control Systems, Embedded Systems, Network Security, Signal Processing

Second Year Project: TSMC 65nm Ring Oscillator for Real-Time Clock IC (Team Lead)

Final Year Project: AI-Driven Optical Authentication Framework using Optical PUFs (Lead Researcher & Developer)

items.name: QuantumID: Deep Spectral Fingerprinting Neural Network
items.description:
Role: Lead Developer & Researcher (Final Year Thesis) | Stack: Python 3.10, PyTorch, NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, scikit-learn

Built an Optical PUF authentication system from EEM fluorescence spectra using a custom convolutional autoencoder. Designed a physics-aware preprocessing pipeline with universal regridding, hybrid normalisation, and 50× physics-based augmentation for low-data regimes. Implemented a two-stage curriculum (reconstruction then contrastive separation) producing a 1536-D latent fingerprint and 1536-bit hash with zero collisions across samples. Achieved 0.966 reconstruction fidelity and sub-50 ms inference for real-time authentication.

items.name: GramSMC & Gram Control Panel: macOS Hardware Control Suite
items.description:
Platform: macOS (Hackintosh) | Tools: DriverKit, macOS Kernel SDK, Objective-C, ACPI/ASL, C++

Developed a comprehensive hardware control suite for LG Gram 13Z990-R laptops on macOS. Engineered custom ACPI tables (SSDT) for hardware device mapping and implemented a DriverKit-based kernel extension. Reverse-engineered LG embedded controller protocols to enable native keyboard backlight control, advanced battery management, and real-time thermal monitoring. Zero kernel panics in 1,000+ hours of testing.

items.name: TSMC 65nm Real-Time Clock Design: Ring Oscillator IC Fabrication Flow
items.description:
Role: Team Lead (6-member team) | Tools: S-Edit, T-Spice, L-Edit, Calibre DRC/LVS

Led complete IC design from RTL to GDSII tape-out for TSMC 65nm. Performed extensive SPICE simulations verifying oscillation frequency stability and timing constraints. Full DRC/LVS verification; submitted GDSII with zero DRC violations. Achieved 15% area reduction through custom cell optimisation and strategic floorplanning.

items.name: 16-Stage FIR Notch Filter for Real-Time Audio on FPGA
items.description:
Platform: Altera Cyclone V (DE1-SoC) | SystemVerilog | Quartus Prime, ModelSim

Designed parametrised FIR digital filter with notch filtering for real-time stereo audio processing. Implemented 4-state FSM controller achieving 19-cycle latency (950 ns at 50 MHz). Processed dual-channel 48 kHz audio with −40 dB notch depth at configurable frequencies. Synthesised design utilised 2,847 logic elements (5% FPGA utilisation).

items.name: WattsApp: Embedded Smart System Management Platform
items.description:
MCU: AVR ATMega644p | Embedded C, InfluxDB, Grafana | UART, I2C

Designed embedded system management platform for real-time monitoring and control. Developed bare-metal C firmware implementing sensor polling, data acquisition, and serial communication for telemetry streaming. Integrated InfluxDB time-series database and Grafana dashboards. Achieved sub-millisecond response times with interrupt-driven architecture.

items.name: MIPSquare++: 5-Stage Pipelined MIPS Processor Simulator
items.description:
Language: C++ (C++23) | CMake, CLion, Git

Developed MIPS CPU simulator implementing 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-Back) with full hazard detection. Custom MIPS assembly parser supporting R-, I-, and J-type instructions. Data forwarding between EX/MEM and MEM/WB stages with load-use stall detection and branch flushing. Achieved near 1 CPI with forwarding enabled.

items.name: Galaxy Book Enabler
items.description:
Language: PowerShell | 10,000+ Downloads | 450+ Stars

Professional-grade Windows tool that spoofs PCs as Samsung Galaxy Books, unlocking Quick Share, Multi Control, Samsung Notes, and 20+ exclusive applications. Implements 21 hardware profiles with registry manipulation, automatic elevation via gsudo, smart package management through WinGet, and auto-update verification.

items.name: eGPU Auto-Enabler
items.description:
Language: PowerShell | PnP Utilities, Power Management APIs

Background service that automatically re-enables external GPUs after hot-plugging on Windows. PnP device monitoring with 2-second polling, custom power plan switching, crash recovery with runtime state preservation, and Windows toast notifications. 99.9% reliability with <50 ms detection latency.

items.name: Enterprise-Grade Home Network Infrastructure
items.description:
Stack: OPNsense, UniFi, Docker Compose, Proxmox VE, HAProxy, Prometheus, Grafana

Architected production-grade home network: OPNsense with Zenarmor IDS/IPS and Tailscale zero-trust VPN; Docker Compose orchestrating 15+ services; Proxmox managing 5 VMs with 3-2-1 backup compliance; Prometheus + Grafana monitoring. Achieved 99.8% uptime over 12 months with zero security incidents and <50 ms average response time serving 8 concurrent users.

items.name: ControlCraft: Interactive Control Systems Analysis Toolbox
items.description:
Platform: MATLAB GUI (App Designer)

Educational tool for control systems analysis: interactive PID tuning with real-time step response visualisation, automated stability analysis (Routh-Hurwitz, Nyquist), and Bode/Nyquist frequency domain plots. Deployed in undergraduate control systems labs serving 60+ students.

items.title: ISACA Winchester Chapter Technical Workshop
items.description: Attended ISACA Winchester Chapter technical workshop on cybersecurity frameworks.

items.title: Open-Source Contributor & Maintainer
items.description: Active open-source contributor and maintainer. Selected public contributions include endlessh-go, Nearby-Sharing-Windows, and AudioPlaybackConnector.

items.title: Engineering Society Technical Demonstrations
items.description: Represented university at Engineering Society technical demonstrations.

items.organization: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)
Information Systems Audit and Control Association (ISACA)
